message.content:
You are an adversarial refuter. ASSUME this finding is FALSE and try to prove it from the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. Find the code path/contract/test that makes the PoC impossible or moot. Only survives=true if you genuinely cannot disprove it.

O-5: Persisted provenance records carry no key/algorithm marker — a keyed (HMAC) trail is indistinguishable from an unkeyed one when re-verified
File: src/assay_engine/provenance.py:211
Description: entries_to_records()/to_records() serialize seq/kind/summary/payload/timestamp/prev_hash/entry_hash but record nothing about whether the chain was sealed with SHA-256 (unkeyed) or HMAC-SHA256 (keyed). from_records()/verify_records() require the caller to supply the correct secret out-of-band; if a verifier omits it (or passes None) on a trail that was actually keyed, verification fails with the generic 'content was altered, or the wrong key was used' error, conflating a missing-key configuration with genuine tampering. Conversely there is no recorded signal that a persisted trail SHOULD have been keyed, so a downstream consumer cannot detect that an audit-grade (cross-trust-boundary) trail was persisted unkeyed. This weakens the provenance-completeness claim for persisted trails.
PoC: trail = ProvenanceTrail(secret=b'x'*16); trail.record('a','b'); recs = trail.to_records(); from_records(recs)  # raises ProvenanceError as if tampered, because no secret was passed and nothing in recs indicates one is required.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll investigate this finding about provenance records not carrying key/algorithm markers.

content.content:
1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, cast
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:
90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return entries_to_records(self._entries)
209	
210	
211	def entries_to_records(
212	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
213	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
214	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
215	
216	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
217	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
218	    """
219	    return tuple(
220	        {
221	            "seq": e.seq,
222	            "kind": e.kind,
223	            "summary": e.summary,
224	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
225	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
226	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
227	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
228	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
229	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
230	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
231	        }
232	        for e in entries
233	    )
234	
235	
236	def verify_records(
237	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
238	    *,
239	    secret: bytes | None = None,
240	) -> None:
241	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
242	
243	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
244	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
245	    """
246	    prev = _GENESIS
247	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
248	        if e.seq != i:
249	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
250	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
251	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
252	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
253	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
254	            raise ProvenanceError(
255	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
256	            )
257	        prev = e.entry_hash
258	
259	
260	def from_records(
261	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
262	    *,
263	    secret: bytes | None = None,
264	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
265	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
266	    _required = ("seq", "kind", "summary", "payload", "timestamp", "prev_hash", "entry_hash")
267	    rebuilt: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
268	    for i, r in enumerate(records):
269	        missing = [k for k in _required if k not in r]
270	        if missing:
271	            # A malformed/incompatible trail record must surface as the module's typed error, not a
272	            # raw KeyError, so a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail file
273	            # covers this case too (pass 4, #G-012).
274	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance record {i} is missing field(s) {missing}")
275	        rebuilt.append(
276	            ProvenanceEntry(
277	                seq=r["seq"],
278	                kind=r["kind"],
279	                summary=r["summary"],
280	                payload=r["payload"],
281	                timestamp=r["timestamp"],
282	                prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
283	                entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
284	            )
285	        )
286	    entries = tuple(rebuilt)
287	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
288	    return entries
289

content.text:
The code confirms the core technical facts. Let me verify the PoC actually behaves as claimed and check whether anything in the serialized records indicates keying.

The serialized record fields are exactly: `seq`, `kind`, `summary`, `payload`, `timestamp`, `prev_hash`, `entry_hash` (lines 220-230). There is no algorithm/key marker. Let me run the PoC to confirm.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && python -c "
from src.assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail, from_records, ProvenanceError
trail = ProvenanceTrail(secret=b'x'*16)
trail.record('a','b')
recs = trail.to_records()
print('record keys:', sorted(recs[0].keys()))
try:
    from_records(recs)
    print('NO ERROR - verified without secret')
except ProvenanceError as e:
    print('ProvenanceError:', e)
"

content.text: PoC confirmed exactly as described. Now let me try to disprove the finding by checking whether something elsewhere actually records the keying status, or whether a contract/test guarantees the secret travels with persisted trails (which would make the "indistinguishable" claim moot).

content.text: Notably, `test_provenance.py:87` asserts that an unkeyed verify of a keyed trail fails — confirming this is documented/expected behavior, not contradicted. Let me check the persistence path in pipeline.py to see whether anything records the keying status alongside the persisted records (which would moot the "indistinguishable" claim).

content.content:
700	        if tuple(visited) != required:
701	            raise FirewallViolation(
702	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
703	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
704	            )
705	        if verify_trail:
706	            # Re-verify the whole chain before handing back the result. This re-hashes every
707	            # entry — O(N) — which is redundant for an in-memory trail this call built and never
708	            # mutated (each entry was hashed correctly when appended). It is a cheap safety net in
709	            # the common case but a real cost for very large trails, so a perf-sensitive caller
710	            # that trusts the in-process trail may pass verify_trail=False (pass 3, #F-036). The
711	            # important re-verification — of a trail deserialized from an external store — is the
712	            # caller's verify_records() on from_records(), which this flag does not affect.
713	            trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
714	        # Terminal success entry (pass 3, #F-006): a phase 'report' record is not a run-level
715	        # terminus, so a trail truncated mid-run is otherwise indistinguishable from one that
716	        # completed. Record run_end ONLY after verify() passes, so its presence as the last
717	        # entry is an affirmative "this run finished with an intact chain" marker. The payload is
718	        # deterministic (no wall-clock) so the provenance-hash determinism guarantee holds — the
719	        # non-deterministic run duration goes to the experiment tracker as an SLO metric instead.
720	        trail.record(
721	            "run_end",
722	            f"study {defn.name!r} completed successfully",
723	            study=defn.name,
724	            experiment_run_id=run_id,
725	            n_provenance_entries=len(trail.entries) + 1,  # +1 counts this terminal entry itself
726	        )
727	        track(_count_logger("run_duration_s", time.monotonic() - started_at))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
728	        # Correlate the hash-chained trail to the experiment run (#G-009): persist it as a JSON
729	        # artifact on the tracker so an auditor opening the run can retrieve the provenance that
730	        # produced its metrics. Best-effort, like the other tracker calls — never aborts the run.
731	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
732

content.text: The persistence path (`_log_trail`, line 733-744) dumps only `entries_to_records(...)` to JSON — no key/algorithm marker, no sidecar indicating the trail was keyed. This confirms the persisted artifact carries no keying signal. Let me check the test that round-trips persisted records (test_pipeline.py:740-747) to confirm it never relies on a marker, and check the keyed test at line 678.

66	# ---- #88: keyed (HMAC) trail is forgery-resistant; unkeyed is honestly only naive-evident ----
67	
68	
69	def test_keyed_trail_cannot_be_reforged_without_secret():
70	    secret = b"provenance-seal-secret-0123456789"
71	    t = ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=_fixed_clock())
72	    t.record("verdict", "H1: SUPPORTED", label="SUPPORTED")
73	    recs = list(t.to_records())
74	    # a forger edits the payload and recomputes the chain — but WITHOUT the secret, using the
75	    # unkeyed path — the keyed verify rejects it.
76	    forged = from_records  # alias
77	    tampered = [{**recs[0], "payload": {"label": "CONTRADICTED"}}]
78	    # recompute as an UNKEYED chain (forger has no secret):
79	    rebuilt = ProvenanceTrail(clock=_fixed_clock())
80	    rebuilt.record("verdict", "H1: CONTRADICTED", label="CONTRADICTED")
81	    forged_recs = rebuilt.to_records()
82	    with pytest.raises(ProvenanceError):
83	        forged(forged_recs, secret=secret)  # does not verify under the real key
84	    # the legitimate keyed trail verifies with the secret, and NOT without it
85	    assert from_records(recs, secret=secret)
86	    with pytest.raises(ProvenanceError):
87	        from_records(recs)  # unkeyed verify of a keyed trail fails
88	    _ = tampered
89

91	def test_weak_secret_refused():
92	    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="16 bytes"):
93	        ProvenanceTrail(secret=b"short")

content.text:
This test confirms the exact behavior the finding describes is the intended/tested contract — there is no marker, and unkeyed verify of a keyed trail fails. The tests reinforce rather than refute the finding.

I have thoroughly attempted to disprove the finding and cannot. Let me confirm my assessment.

The finding's three claims are all verified against the code:

1. **No key/algorithm marker in serialized records** — `entries_to_records()` (lines 220-230) emits exactly `seq/kind/summary/payload/timestamp/prev_hash/entry_hash`. Confirmed by running the PoC: record keys are exactly those seven. No HMAC/SHA-256/keyed flag.

2. **PoC raises generic tamper error** — Running `from_records(recs)` on a keyed trail without the secret raises `ProvenanceError: provenance entry 0 content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)` — verbatim conflation of missing-key vs. tampering (provenance.py:254-256).

3. **No signal that a persisted trail SHOULD be keyed** — The persistence path in `pipeline.py:733-744` (`_log_trail`) dumps only `entries_to_records(...)` to JSON with no sidecar/marker. A downstream consumer cannot tell an audit-grade trail was persisted unkeyed.

Attempts to refute, all failed:
- No marker field anywhere in the serialization or any sidecar.
- The secret does not travel with persisted records (it's passed out-of-band to `verify_records`/`from_records`).
- `test_provenance.py:86-87` and `test_pipeline.py:680` actively assert that unkeyed verify of a keyed trail fails — the behavior is the *tested, intended contract*, so there's no hidden mechanism that mitigates it.

The only nuance: the module docstring (lines 24-28) and `verify_records` docstring (line 244) explicitly state the secret must be supplied out-of-band, so this is a *documented* design choice rather than an oversight. But the finding is about an absent capability (no recorded marker), and that absence is real and has the described consequence for persisted, cross-process trails. Documentation of a limitation does not make the limitation moot.

input.reasoning:
Verified against /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/provenance.py and confirmed by executing the PoC with the project's .venv python. All three claims hold and I could not disprove any.

1. No key/algorithm marker: entries_to_records() (provenance.py:219-233) serializes exactly seq/kind/summary/payload/timestamp/prev_hash/entry_hash. PoC output confirms record keys are precisely those seven — nothing distinguishes an HMAC-SHA256 (keyed) chain from a SHA-256 (unkeyed) one.

2. Generic error conflates missing-key with tampering: from_records(recs) on a trail built with secret=b'x'*16 raised "ProvenanceError: provenance entry 0 content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)" (provenance.py:252-256), verbatim as the finding states.

3. No signal a persisted trail SHOULD be keyed: the persistence path in pipeline.py _log_trail (lines 733-744) dumps only entries_to_records(...) as JSON to the experiment tracker — no sidecar, no flag. A downstream auditor cannot detect that an audit-grade trail was persisted unkeyed.

Refutation attempts (all failed): (a) searched src/ and tests/ for any marker/algo/keyed field or sidecar carrying the secret or algorithm — none exists; the secret is always passed out-of-band to verify_records/from_records. (b) The behavior is not an accidental gap that some other code path covers — it is the explicitly tested contract: test_provenance.py:86-87 (from_records(recs) without secret on a keyed trail raises) and test_pipeline.py:680 assert exactly this failure mode, so there is no hidden mitigation. (c) The module docstring (lines 16-28) and verify_records docstring (line 244) document that the caller must supply the secret out-of-band, but documenting the limitation does not make the absent marker or the error conflation untrue. The weakness to the provenance-completeness claim for persisted trails is real.

content.text: Finding O-5 survives. The PoC reproduces exactly as described, and I could not find any code path, contract, or test that makes it false — on the contrary, the existing tests (`test_provenance.py:86-87`, `test_pipeline.py:680`) codify the exact conflation behavior as intended, and the persistence path (`pipeline.py:733-744`) writes the records with no key/algorithm marker.
